analysis.reasoning: Core message: Lavrov addresses youth, expressing confidence they will make Russia better, safer, and more advanced, and reflects on the heritage the current generation will leave. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): Not referenced; no mention of the West or hostility. RU2 (Russia leads alternative system): No discussion of multipolarity, BRICS, or alternative order; not applicable. RU3 (Russia as benevolent partner): No reference to international partnerships, peace mediation, or solidarity abroad; not applicable. RU4 (Russian history is admirable): While 'heritage' is mentioned, there is no celebration of past historical achievements; focus is future-oriented and domestic, so not applicable. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): The phrasing "more advanced" aspires to advancement but does not showcase current successes or leadership; aspirational, not evidentiary, so not applicable. RU6 (Russia’s culture/nature/heritage): The word "heritage" appears, but there is no promotion of culture, traditions, or natural beauty; not applicable. Conclusion: The tweet is generic motivational rhetoric without specific alignment to listed narratives.
